About Tempe Arizona
If you are planning to attend Arizona State University, finding student apartments in Tempe becomes necessary. Located in Maricopa County, Arizona, Tempe is home to over 200,000 residents. Among these, over 74,000 are students.
Tempe has a dry and hot desert climate with almost 4 months of summer. Even so, this college town is popular among students from across the world due to one of the largest universities in the US. Named after the Vale of Tempe of ancient Greece, the city was originally an agricultural town until World War II. Now, Tempe is the seventh-largest city in Arizona.

Room Types Available in Tempe
You can select from a range of room types while choosing for student apartments in Tempe. From studios for better privacy to shared rooms for never being alone, Tempe student rooms come in a variety of layouts. Here are the most popular ones.
1. Bedroom Apartments:
When you want a room all to yourself, but also need a small group of friends waiting on you when you get home late, choose bedroom apartments. Choose from 2-, 3-, 4-, or 5-bedroom layouts to share with a small or large group.
2. Studios:

Apartment: Various ASU Apartments | Source: Amber Student | Date: 2026
2. Studios:
If you’re someone whose social battery drains as the sun sets, studios might be a good option for you. These are individual units with a private bedroom, private kitchen, and private bathroom, all to yourself.
3. 1-Bedroom Apartments:
Want to live alone but also want to call friends over for a sleepover? 1-Bedroom apartments are perfect. These give you a private bedroom, a living room, a private kitchen, and a private bath.
4. Private Room:
A private room type can be available as a studio or in a shared apartment, too. With a private room, you may have an ensuite or a non-ensuite.
5. Shared Room:
Shared rooms are the least private of all the other options. These can be part of a studio or a shared-bedroom apartment. Shared rooms reduce rent and let you live comfortably while paying significantly less for the same room.

Student Travel In Tempe
Tempe offers a wide range of transportation options for students to choose from to travel across the city. These options vary from free to minimal fare with a monthly pass. Here’s all you need to know about student travel options in Tempe.
1. Valley Metro Bus:
The buses run on all the major bus routes across the city. A regular bus ride will cost you at least $2, or you can get a monthly pass for $64.
2. Tempe ORBIT:
This is a free shuttle that runs across 6 routes between Downtown Tempe and the ASU campus.
3. Light Rail:
METRO Light Rail is a great option for students who want to travel to Central Phoenix or Mesa quickly. The light rail also connects downtown to Sky Harbor Airport for students planning their journey further.
4. Bike:
As a cycling-friendly city, Tempe encourages students to ride bikes with 220 miles of bike lanes throughout the city.

ASU Tempe Student Apartment Review: Apollo Tempe (Updated for 2026)
By Maddy
If you’re an ASU student looking for your next place to call home, you may feel overwhelmed by all the student apartments available in Tempe.
While we feel that Rambler Tempe’s diverse floor plan options, top-of-the-line amenities, and premium location will check all the boxes on any ASU student’s checklist, we understand the importance of comparing all of your options before making a final decision.
To help make this comparison process easier, we have written this article reviewing another student apartment, Apollo Tempe.
Photo courtesy of Apollo Tempe.
The Basics
First, we’ll review some of the basics of the property.`
Address:
1100 E Apache Blvd
Year Opened:
2020
Number of Units:
327
Number of Stories:
6
Location
Apollo Tempe is just half a mile from ASU’s campus and 1.5 miles from Mill Avenue District. It’s close to campus buildings like the Memorial Union and the Sun Devil Fitness Complex.
Additionally, Apollo Tempe is near popular dining options, including Chutneys Indian Express, Voodoo Donuts, Someburros, Honey 2 A Bee and Taco Bell.
READ NEXT:
Student Housing vs. Traditional Apartments Near ASU
Amenities
Photo courtesy of Apollo Tempe.
At Apollo Tempe, residents have access to a variety of amenities including a rooftop pool with a spa, fire pits and grills, a ground-level pool with infinity waterfall, rooftop and ground level fitness centers, a yoga studio, a rock wall, two clubrooms, study lounges, an outdoor basketball court, a zen area and dog park.
Parking

Apartment: Apollo Tempe | Source: RamblerTempe | Date: 2026
Parking
If you were planning on bringing a car to ASU, Apollo Tempe has onsite parking available for purchase on a first come, first serve basis for an additional monthly fee of $125.

Pros and Cons of Bringing a Car to Campus at ASU
Pricing and Floor Plans
Photo courtesy of Apollo Tempe.
Apollo Tempe has floor plans ranging from 1×1 to 4×4. Every apartment has stainless steel appliances, granite countertops, a washer and dryer and wifi.
As of publication in February 2026, monthly rental rates range anywhere from $1,199 to $1,929 depending on the floor plan. Rates may vary throughout the lease-up season, as student housing communities typically use a tiered pricing system, with rates increasing as leases are signed. This means signing early can help you lock in lower rates before they increase closer to the school year.
Apollo Tempe is a pet-friendly community with the exception of large dogs. As of February 2026, if residents chose to bring a pet, they will pay a one-time non-refundable pet deposit of $300 and a recurring pet rent of $50 per month.
We hope this information helps you decide where the best place is for you near ASU in Tempe. If you have any further questions or would like to learn more about Rambler Tempe, pay us a visit at Rambler House or
contact our leasing staff.
We’re happy to assist you in your search for housing!

on Renting:
1. It took some time to find but luckily I had a roommate that found it!
2. Had to start searching for housing very early and had to begin renting in July to secure
residence.

student).
4. I needed to make a deposit before securing my off-campus housing.
5. I found my housing through Facebook pages and WhatsApp groups.
6. It was hard knowing how accessible complexes were in comparison to campus. It was very
difficult navigating finding housing without being in Phoenix. I did not know all my
options.
7. In some apartment locations, you do not have to have roommates to get the multiroom
apartments where the rent is less expensive. The apartment can pair you up with other
students.
8. Apply as soon as possible as places fill up very quickly!
9. Try to meet your roommates before planning to live with them.
10. Rent is higher in downtown Phoenix, but the benefit is you can walk to class!

Maintaining a healthy and secure campus community for students, staff, and faculty

Escort Service on this app is only available on the ASU Tempe campus.

If you’re looking for a place to live in Tempe near ASU for the next school year, you might be overwhelmed by all of the housing options available.

Union Tempe offers both individual rates and full unit rates depending on unit type. As of publication in February 2026, monthly rates start from $889 per person, per month for private rental to full unit pricing up to $1,450 depending on unit type.
